key_models.ir.module.module: Module registry. Fields: name, state, version, dependencies_id.
key_models.ir.model: Model registry. Fields: model, name, state, field_id, access_ids.
key_models.ir.model.fields: Field registry. Fields: name, model_id, ttype, relation, compute, store.
key_models.ir.ui.view: View definitions. Fields: name, model, type, arch, inherit_id, mode.
key_models.ir.actions.act_window: Window actions. Fields: name, res_model, view_mode, domain, context.
key_models.ir.actions.report: Report actions. Fields: name, model, report_type, report_name, paperformat_id.

key_files.odoo/http.py: Controller base classes and routing decorators
key_files.odoo/tests/common.py: Test base classes TransactionCase and HttpCase

security_groups.base.group_system: Administration / Settings — install modules and edit views
security_groups.base.group_no_one: Technical Features — technical menus and fields

best_practices: Always define __manifest__.py with explicit dependencies and correct version
Follow Odoo naming conventions: models in models/, views in views/, security in security/
Use _name for new models and _inherit for extending existing models
Prefer computed stored fields over onchange for business logic
Use @api.constrains for validation and @api.depends for computed fields
Write views with xpath inheritance for upstream compatibility
Implement controllers with correct auth and CSRF settings
Build OWL components with proper props and reactive state
Create QWeb reports with minimal logic in templates
Write tests using TransactionCase and tag with @tagged('post_install', '-at_install') when needed
Use pre-commit hooks with flake8, pylint, and black for code quality
Keep business logic in models, not in views or controllers
Use Odoo shell for debugging and one-off data operations
